intro: The most concrete split between Iugu and Maxio is the pricing model: Iugu runs on usage-based pricing, while Maxio uses subscription from $100. That shapes how predictable your monthly spend is and how much of the cost sits in infrastructure versus per-seat or per-event fees. Beyond the headline, Iugu carries medium lock-in (brazil billing platform) and usage-based pricing; Maxio carries medium lock-in (billing data exportable) and subscription from $100. Iugu is a fit when you are brazilian saas, marketplaces, and fintechs needing pix, boleto, and nf-e compliance. Maxio is a fit when you are saas. The honest trade-off: Iugu is explicitly not for global-first saas or companies without significant brazilian revenue exposure; Maxio is explicitly not for simple projects. On the product side, Iugu is described as Brazilian billing and payments platform for subscriptions, recurring charges, and marketplaces with REST API, and Maxio is described as Subscription and revenue management platform for B2B SaaS combining billing, revenue recognition, and financial metrics.
quick_take: Iugu is for brazilian saas, marketplaces, and fintechs needing pix, boleto, and nf-e compliance; Maxio is for saas; decide on pricing model.
verdict_a: Choose Iugu if its usage pricing model fits your budget shape better than Maxio's subscription model, and you are brazilian saas, marketplaces, and fintechs needing pix, boleto, and nf-e compliance.
verdict_b: Choose Maxio if its subscription pricing model fits your budget shape better than Iugu's usage model, and you are saas.

faq.a: Both tools use usage pricing. Iugu is listed as usage-based pricing; Maxio as subscription from $100. Without specific volume and seat assumptions it is not possible to say which is cheaper in general.

faq.q: Can I migrate from Iugu to Maxio?
faq.a: Migration from Iugu to Maxio is feasible but not trivial: Iugu has medium lock-in (brazil billing platform). Core data can move; integrations and workflow logic need rebuild. Allow for a parallel-run period.

On our scoring Maxio rates 4/5 for developer experience versus 3/5 for Iugu. That suggests Maxio feels more polished for everyday developer tasks. We still recommend a short hands-on trial on your stack before standardising.

faq.q: Is Maxio a good alternative to Iugu?
faq.a: Maxio is pitched at saas, which overlaps with Iugu's fit (brazilian saas, marketplaces, and fintechs needing pix, boleto, and nf-e compliance) but is not identical. Note that Maxio is explicitly not for simple projects, so it only replaces Iugu if you are outside that exclusion. Pricing models differ (usage vs subscription), which may shift total cost.
